This might include the\\n106:      function name (a symbol), its parameters, and the block that\\n107:      makes up the function's body, and the local variables of the\\n108:      function.\\n109: \\n110:      This is only called for FUNCTION_DECLs. Called from the parser\\n120:      after the parsing process has finished.\\n121: \\n122:      This gets called for both variables and functions.\\n123: \\n124:      Location information is not available at this point, but it is a\\n125:      good probe point to get access to symbols before they get\\n126:      optimized away.\\n127: \\n128:      This hook may be called on VAR_DECLs or FUNCTION_DECLs.  It is up\\n129:      to the hook to use what it needs.  */\\n130:   void (* early_global_decl) (tree decl);\\n131: \\n132:   /* Augment debug information generated by early_global_decl with\\n133:      more complete debug info (if applicable). die || ! child_die)\\n5775:     return;\\n5776:   gcc_assert (die != child_die);\\n5777: \\n5778:   child_die->die_parent = die;\\n5779:   if (die->die_child)\\n5780:     {\\n5781:       child_die->die_sib = die->die_child->die_sib;\\n5782:       die->die_child->die_sib = child_die;\\n5783:     }\\n5784:   else\\n5785:     child_die->die_sib = child_die;\\n5786:   die->die_child = child_die;\\n5787: }\\n5788: \\n5789: /* Like add_child_die, but put CHILD_DIE after AFTER_DIE.  */\\n5790: \\n5791: static void\\n5792: add_child_die_after (dw_die_ref die, dw_die_ref child_die,\\n5793: \\t\\t     dw_die_ref after_die)\\n5794: {\\n5795:   gcc_assert (die\\n5796: \\t      && child_die\\n5797: \\t      && after_die\\n5798: \\t      && die->die_child\\n5799: \\t      && die != child_die);\\n5800: \\n5801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333@baaf9b33ef307e3d2752a94e62274c374e6a5e20/-/blob/gcc/dwarf2out.cc%3FL5700-5800\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01RMzVCHV9X2eXmMHvn7ntYM","session_id":"0be8946d-582b-4e49-bf3a-6e51e251d74b","uuid":"8d6d0cb7-ae70-438e-900b-7b51ff312043"}

This\\n25958:      can happen for character types in Ada for instance: the character type\\n25959:      used for code generation can be signed, to be compatible with the C one,\\n25960:      but from a debugger point of view, it must be unsigned.  */\\n25961:   bool is_orig_unsigned = TYPE_UNSIGNED (TREE_TYPE (src));\\n25962:   bool is_debug_unsigned = TYPE_UNSIGNED (discr_type);\\n25963: \\n25964:   if (is_orig_unsigned != is_debug_unsigned)\\n25965:     src = fold_convert (discr_type, src);\\n25966: \\n25967:   if (!(is_debug_unsigned ? tree_fits_uhwi_p (src) : tree_fits_shwi_p (src)))\\n25968:     return false;\\n25969: \\n25970:   dest->pos = is_debug_unsigned;\\n25971:   if (is_debug_unsigned)\\n25972:     dest->v.uval = tree_to_uhwi (src);\\n25973:   else\\n25974:     dest->v.sval = tree_to_shwi (src);\\n25975: \\n25976:   return true;\\n25977: }\\n25978: \\n25979: /* Try to extract synthetic properties out of VARIANT_PART_DECL, which is a\\n25980:    FIELD_DECL in STRUCT_TYPE that represents a variant part. Otherwise:\\n25982: \\n25983:      - store the discriminant field in STRUCT_TYPE that controls the variant\\n25984:        part to *DISCR_DECL\\n25985: \\n25986:      - put in *DISCR_LISTS_P an array where for each variant, the item\\n25987:        represents the corresponding matching list of discriminant values.\\n25988: \\n25989:      - put in *DISCR_LISTS_LENGTH the number of variants, which is the size of\\n25990:        the above array.\\n25991: \\n25992:    Note that when the array is allocated (i.e. when the analysis is\\n25993:    successful), it is up to the caller to free the array.  */\\n25994: \\n25995: static void\\n25996: analyze_variants_discr (tree variant_part_decl,\\n25997: \\t\\t\\ttree struct_type,\\n25998: \\t\\t\\ttree *discr_decl,\\n25999: \\t\\t\\tdw_discr_list_ref **discr_lists_p,\\n26000: \\t\\t\\tunsigned *discr_lists_length)\\n26001: {\\n26002:   tree variant_part_type = TREE_TYPE (variant_part_decl);\\n26003:   tree variant;\\n26004:   dw_discr_list_ref *discr_lists;\\n26005:   unsigned i;\\n26006: \\n26007:   /* Compute how many variants there are in this variant part.  */\\n26008:   *discr_lists_length = 0;\\n26009:   for (variant = TYPE_FIELDS (variant_part_type);\\n26010:        variant != NULL_TREE;\\n26011:        variant = DECL_CHAIN (variant))\\n26012:     ++*discr_lists_length;\\n26013: \\n26014:   *discr_decl = NULL_TREE;\\n26015:   *discr_lists_p\\n26016:     = (dw_discr_list_ref *) xcalloc (*discr_lists_length,\\n26017: \\t\\t\\t\\t     sizeof (**discr_lists_p));\\n26018:   discr_lists = *discr_lists_p;\\n26019: \\n26020:   /* And then analyze all variants to extract discriminant information for all\\n26021:      of them.  This analysis is conservative: as soon as we detect something we\\n26022:      do not support, abort everything and pretend we found nothing.  */\\n26023:   for (variant = TYPE_FIELDS (variant_part_type), i = 0;\\n26024:        variant != NULL_TREE;\\n26025:        variant = DECL_CHAIN (variant), ++i)\\n26026:     {\\n26027:       tree match_expr = DECL_QUALIFIER (variant);\\n26028: \\n26029:       /* Now, try to analyze the predicate and deduce a discriminant for\\n26030: \\t it.  */\\n26031:       if (match_expr == boolean_true_node)\\n26032: \\t/* Typically happens for the default variant: it matches all cases that\\n26033: \\t   previous variants rejected.  Don't output any matching value for\\n26034: \\t   this one.  */\\n26035: \\tcontinue;\\n26036: \\n26037:       /* The following loop tries to iterate over each discriminant\\n26038: \\t possibility: single values or ranges.  */\\n26039:       while (match_expr != NULL_TREE)\\n26040: \\t{\\n26041: \\t  tree next_round_match_expr;\\n26042: \\t  tree candidate_discr = NULL_TREE;\\n26043: \\t  dw_discr_list_ref new_node = NULL;\\n26044: \\n26045: \\t  /* Possibilities are matched one after the other by nested\\n26046: \\t     TRUTH_ORIF_EXPR expressions. default:\n30463:         gcc_unreachable ();\n30464:     }\n30465:   return 1;\n30466: }\n30467: \n30468: /* A helper function for dwarf2out_finish.   \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2asm.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dw2_asm_output_data\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"# GCC DWARF Debug Information Generation Chain\\n\\n## Overview\\nThe GCC DWARF debug information generation is structured in a multi-phase architecture where the front-end communicates with the debug back-end through a hook interface (`gcc_debug_hooks`), which is implemented by the DWARF2 subsystem.\\n\\n## Key Architecture Components\\n\\n### 1. Debug Hook Interface (gcc/debug.h)\\nThe `struct gcc_debug_hooks` defined in `gcc/debug.h` is the primary interface between the compiler front-end and the DWARF debug information generator. It includes hooks for:\\n\\n- **Initialization/Finalization**: `init()`, `finish()`, `early_finish()`\\n- **Function/Variable Processing**: `early_global_decl()`, `late_global_decl()`, `function_decl()`\\n- **Type Information**: `type_decl()`\\n- **Lexical Blocks**: `begin_block()`, `end_block()`, `ignore_block()`\\n- **Source Location Tracking**: `source_line()`, `begin_prologue()`, `end_prologue()`, `var_location()`\\n- **Module/Declaration Imports**: `imported_module_or_decl()`\\n- **Preprocessing Information**: `define()`, `undef()`, `start_source_file()`, `end_source_file()`\\n\\nThe global instance `debug_hooks` is set at runtime (in `gcc/toplev.cc`) to point to `dwarf2_debug_hooks` based on debugging options.\\n\\n### 2. DWARF DIE Creation (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nDEBUG Information Entries (DIEs) are created through a two-level mechanism:\\n\\n- **`new_die_raw(enum dwarf_tag tag)`**: Low-level DIE allocation that creates an uninitialized `struct die_struct` with the specified DWARF tag. Located in `gcc/dwarf2out.cc`.\\n\\n- **`new_die(enum dwarf_tag tag, dw_die_ref parent_die, tree t)`**: Higher-level DIE creation that:\\n  - Calls `new_die_raw()` to allocate the DIE\\n  - Adds the DIE as a child to its parent via `add_child_die()`\\n  - Handles \\\"limbo DIEs\\\" (parentless DIEs) using a special limbo die list\\n  - Associates the DIE with tree nodes via lookup tables\\n\\n- **`add_child_die(dw_die_ref parent_die, dw_die_ref child_die)`**: Adds a DIE as the last child of another DIE, maintaining the DIE tree structure.\\n\\nThe `dw_die_ref` typedef (defined in `gcc/dwarf2out.h`) is a pointer to `struct die_struct`, the internal representation of a DWARF Debug Info Entry.\\n\\n### 3. DWARF Output Generation (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nOnce DIEs are created, the DWARF sections are generated during the `dwarf2out_finish()` function, which:\\n\\n- **`output_comp_unit(dw_die_ref die, unsigned int depth, dw_die_ref *checksum)`**: Outputs a complete compilation unit\\n  - Calls `output_die()` recursively to output all DIEs in the unit\\n  - Handles abbreviation table generation\\n  - Manages DWARF section output\\n\\n- **`output_die(dw_die_ref die)`**: Recursively outputs a DIE and all its attributes:\\n  - Emits the DIE tag and attributes\\n  - Calls itself recursively for child DIEs\\n  - Uses attribute output functions to serialize DIE data\\n\\n### 4. Line Number Table Emission (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nSource location information is tracked and output via:\\n\\n- **`new_line_info_table()`**: Creates a new `dw_line_info_table` structure for a section, initializing line number state machine parameters (file number, line number, statement marker, view, etc.)\\n\\n- **`set_cur_line_info_table(section *sec)`**: Sets the current line info table for a specific section (text_section, cold_text_section, or other sections)\\n\\n- **`output_line_info(bool prologue_only)`**: Main function for outputting the `.debug_line` section:\\n  - Generates the line number program header (version, minimum instruction length, etc.)\\n  - Outputs the file names table\\n  - Outputs the line number program state machine instructions\\n\\n- **`output_one_line_info_table(dw_line_info_table *table)`**: Outputs a single line number table into the `.debug_line` section:\\n  - Encodes source line number information using the line number program state machine\\n  - Handles address setting (LI_set_address)\\n  - Handles line number updates (LI_set_line)\\n  - Handles file number changes (LI_set_file)\\n  - Handles column information (LI_set_column)\\n  - Handles discriminator values (LI_set_discriminator)\\n\\n### 5. Runtime Hook Dispatch (gcc/toplev.cc)\\nThe debug hooks are initialized in `toplev.cc`:\\n- The global `debug_hooks` pointer is assigned to `&dwarf2_debug_hooks` when DWARF2 debugging is enabled\\n- The `dwarf2_debug_hooks` structure contains function pointers to all DWARF2-specific implementations\\n- Hook implementations include functions like `dwarf2out_init()`, `dwarf2out_early_global_decl()`, `dwarf2out_source_line()`, etc.\\n\\n## Execution Flow\\n\\n1. **Early Debug Phase**: When `dwarf2out_init()` is called, it initializes data structures for DIE creation and early compilation unit setup\\n\\n2. **Compilation Phase**: As the compiler processes declarations and definitions, front-end calls debug hooks:\\n   - `dwarf2out_early_global_decl()` for declarations\\n   - `dwarf2out_late_global_decl()` for variable/function definitions with full information\\n   - `dwarf2out_function_decl()` for function definitions\\n   - `dwarf2out_source_line()` for source location tracking\\n   - `dwarf2out_var_location()` for variable location information\\n\\n3. **DIE Creation**: Debug hooks create DIEs using `new_die()` and `new_die_raw()`, building a tree structure via `add_child_die()`\\n\\n4. **Finish Phase**: At end of compilation, `dwarf2out_finish()` orchestrates:\\n   - Walking all created DIEs\\n   - Outputting compilation units via `output_comp_unit()`\\n   - Outputting DIEs recursively via `output_die()`\\n   - Generating the line number table via `output_line_info()`\\n\\n## Supporting Infrastructure\\n\\n- **gcc/dwarf2asm.cc**: Low-level DWARF assembly output functions (`dw2_asm_output_data()`, etc.)\\n- **gcc/dwarf2cfi.cc**: Call Frame Information (CFI) generation for exception handling\\n- **gcc/dwarf2ctf.cc**: CTF (Compact Type Format) debug info generation from DWARF DIEs\\n- **gcc/ctfc.cc**: CTF container management and type definition tracking\\n- **gcc/final.cc**: Final assembly phase, calls debug hooks for prologue/epilogue tracking\\n\\nThis architecture allows GCC to generate complete DWARF2 debug information through a clean hook-based interface, enabling support for multiple debug formats while maintaining a consistent internal representation based on DWARF DIEs.\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2821,"cache_read_input_tokens":114747,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2821},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0be8946d-582b-4e49-bf3a-6e51e251d74b","uuid":"b7b7e600-caff-470f-aaf6-37830f5ea533"}

  \"repo\": \"github.com/sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333\",\n      \"path\": \"gcc/dwarf2asm.cc\",\n      \"symbol\": \"dw2_asm_output_data\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"# GCC DWARF Debug Information Generation Chain\\n\\n## Overview\\nThe GCC DWARF debug information generation is structured in a multi-phase architecture where the front-end communicates with the debug back-end through a hook interface (`gcc_debug_hooks`), which is implemented by the DWARF2 subsystem.\\n\\n## Key Architecture Components\\n\\n### 1. Debug Hook Interface (gcc/debug.h)\\nThe `struct gcc_debug_hooks` defined in `gcc/debug.h` is the primary interface between the compiler front-end and the DWARF debug information generator. It includes hooks for:\\n\\n- **Initialization/Finalization**: `init()`, `finish()`, `early_finish()`\\n- **Function/Variable Processing**: `early_global_decl()`, `late_global_decl()`, `function_decl()`\\n- **Type Information**: `type_decl()`\\n- **Lexical Blocks**: `begin_block()`, `end_block()`, `ignore_block()`\\n- **Source Location Tracking**: `source_line()`, `begin_prologue()`, `end_prologue()`, `var_location()`\\n- **Module/Declaration Imports**: `imported_module_or_decl()`\\n- **Preprocessing Information**: `define()`, `undef()`, `start_source_file()`, `end_source_file()`\\n\\nThe global instance `debug_hooks` is set at runtime (in `gcc/toplev.cc`) to point to `dwarf2_debug_hooks` based on debugging options.\\n\\n### 2. DWARF DIE Creation (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nDEBUG Information Entries (DIEs) are created through a two-level mechanism:\\n\\n- **`new_die_raw(enum dwarf_tag tag)`**: Low-level DIE allocation that creates an uninitialized `struct die_struct` with the specified DWARF tag. Located in `gcc/dwarf2out.cc`.\\n\\n- **`new_die(enum dwarf_tag tag, dw_die_ref parent_die, tree t)`**: Higher-level DIE creation that:\\n  - Calls `new_die_raw()` to allocate the DIE\\n  - Adds the DIE as a child to its parent via `add_child_die()`\\n  - Handles \\\"limbo DIEs\\\" (parentless DIEs) using a special limbo die list\\n  - Associates the DIE with tree nodes via lookup tables\\n\\n- **`add_child_die(dw_die_ref parent_die, dw_die_ref child_die)`**: Adds a DIE as the last child of another DIE, maintaining the DIE tree structure.\\n\\nThe `dw_die_ref` typedef (defined in `gcc/dwarf2out.h`) is a pointer to `struct die_struct`, the internal representation of a DWARF Debug Info Entry.\\n\\n### 3. DWARF Output Generation (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nOnce DIEs are created, the DWARF sections are generated during the `dwarf2out_finish()` function, which:\\n\\n- **`output_comp_unit(dw_die_ref die, unsigned int depth, dw_die_ref *checksum)`**: Outputs a complete compilation unit\\n  - Calls `output_die()` recursively to output all DIEs in the unit\\n  - Handles abbreviation table generation\\n  - Manages DWARF section output\\n\\n- **`output_die(dw_die_ref die)`**: Recursively outputs a DIE and all its attributes:\\n  - Emits the DIE tag and attributes\\n  - Calls itself recursively for child DIEs\\n  - Uses attribute output functions to serialize DIE data\\n\\n### 4. Line Number Table Emission (gcc/dwarf2out.cc)\\nSource location information is tracked and output via:\\n\\n- **`new_line_info_table()`**: Creates a new `dw_line_info_table` structure for a section, initializing line number state machine parameters (file number, line number, statement marker, view, etc.)\\n\\n- **`set_cur_line_info_table(section *sec)`**: Sets the current line info table for a specific section (text_section, cold_text_section, or other sections)\\n\\n- **`output_line_info(bool prologue_only)`**: Main function for outputting the `.debug_line` section:\\n  - Generates the line number program header (version, minimum instruction length, etc.)\\n  - Outputs the file names table\\n  - Outputs the line number program state machine instructions\\n\\n- **`output_one_line_info_table(dw_line_info_table *table)`**: Outputs a single line number table into the `.debug_line` section:\\n  - Encodes source line number information using the line number program state machine\\n  - Handles address setting (LI_set_address)\\n  - Handles line number updates (LI_set_line)\\n  - Handles file number changes (LI_set_file)\\n  - Handles column information (LI_set_column)\\n  - Handles discriminator values (LI_set_discriminator)\\n\\n### 5. Runtime Hook Dispatch (gcc/toplev.cc)\\nThe debug hooks are initialized in `toplev.cc`:\\n- The global `debug_hooks` pointer is assigned to `&dwarf2_debug_hooks` when DWARF2 debugging is enabled\\n- The `dwarf2_debug_hooks` structure contains function pointers to all DWARF2-specific implementations\\n- Hook implementations include functions like `dwarf2out_init()`, `dwarf2out_early_global_decl()`, `dwarf2out_source_line()`, etc.\\n\\n## Execution Flow\\n\\n1. **Early Debug Phase**: When `dwarf2out_init()` is called, it initializes data structures for DIE creation and early compilation unit setup\\n\\n2. **Compilation Phase**: As the compiler processes declarations and definitions, front-end calls debug hooks:\\n   - `dwarf2out_early_global_decl()` for declarations\\n   - `dwarf2out_late_global_decl()` for variable/function definitions with full information\\n   - `dwarf2out_function_decl()` for function definitions\\n   - `dwarf2out_source_line()` for source location tracking\\n   - `dwarf2out_var_location()` for variable location information\\n\\n3. **DIE Creation**: Debug hooks create DIEs using `new_die()` and `new_die_raw()`, building a tree structure via `add_child_die()`\\n\\n4. **Finish Phase**: At end of compilation, `dwarf2out_finish()` orchestrates:\\n   - Walking all created DIEs\\n   - Outputting compilation units via `output_comp_unit()`\\n   - Outputting DIEs recursively via `output_die()`\\n   - Generating the line number table via `output_line_info()`\\n\\n## Supporting Infrastructure\\n\\n- **gcc/dwarf2asm.cc**: Low-level DWARF assembly output functions (`dw2_asm_output_data()`, etc.)\\n- **gcc/dwarf2cfi.cc**: Call Frame Information (CFI) generation for exception handling\\n- **gcc/dwarf2ctf.cc**: CTF (Compact Type Format) debug info generation from DWARF DIEs\\n- **gcc/ctfc.cc**: CTF container management and type definition tracking\\n- **gcc/final.cc**: Final assembly phase, calls debug hooks for prologue/epilogue tracking\\n\\nThis architecture allows GCC to generate complete DWARF2 debug information through a clean hook-based interface, enabling support for multiple debug formats while maintaining a consistent internal representation based on DWARF DIEs.\"\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
